name: Spicy Duck Casserole
recipeIngredient: 2 medium red onions, cut in half
2 medium yellow onions, cut in half
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove giblets and neck from duckling; refrigerate to use in soup another day. Cut duckling into quarters; trim excess skin and fat. Rinse duckling with running cold water; pat dry with paper towels.
2. In an 8-quart Dutch oven over high heat, brown duckling pieces; remove pieces to a large bowl as they brown. Discard all but 1 tablespoon of the fat from the Dutch oven.
3. In the remaining fat in the Dutch oven, cook onion halves until golden brown. Remove onions to the bowl with duckling.
4. Into the same Dutch oven, stir soy sauce, sherry, grated ginger root, sugar, star anise, and 3/4 cup water. Over high heat, heat to boiling, stirring to loosen any brown bits from the bottom of the Dutch oven.
5. Return duckling pieces and onions to the Dutch oven. Cover and bake in a 350 degrees F. oven for 1 1/4 hours. Meanwhile, trim ends from Brussels sprouts. Cut Brussels sprouts in half if they are large. Stir Brussels sprouts into the liquid in the Dutch oven. Cover and bake for an additional 45 minutes or until duckling and vegetables are tender, basting duckling and vegetables with liquid several times during baking.
6. To serve, skim fat from the liquid in the Dutch oven.
